TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON NOV-20-2015 IN 503, CONTINUED HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT - HEARING HELD. THE COURT HEARD AND MADE THE FOLLOWING RULING: (1) THE COURT FINDS THAT COUNSEL JESSICA R. BARSOTTI AND DEFENDANT DEAN GREGORY ASIMOS DID NOT APPEAR AT THE NOVEMBER 13, 2015 HEARING AS ORDERED PREVIOUSLY. FURTHERMORE DEFENDANT ASIMOS DID NOT FULLY COMPLY WITH THE PREVIOUS COURT ORDER TO PRODUCE THE SIGNED RELEASE OF THE FUNDS HELD IN THE CARR MCCLELLAN TRUST ACCOUNT. THE COURT FINDS DEFENDANT ASIMOS IN CONTEMPT FOR FAILURE TO APPEAR AND A VIOLATION OF A COURT ORDER. (2) THE COURT ORDERS THE STAY OF THE $1,000.00 CONTEMPT FINE AS TO MR. DEAN GREGORY ASIMOS LIFTED AND THE FINE IMPOSED AS OF THIS DATE. DEFENDANT HAS UNTIL JANUARY 4, 2016 BY 4:00 P.M. TO DELIVER A CHECK OF $1,000 TO THE CLERK OF THE SUPERIOR COURT, COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. THE COURT, ON ITS OWN MOTION, ORDERS TO RECALL OF THE BENCH WARRANT AS TO MR. ASIMOS ISSUED ON NOVEMBER 17, 2015. (3) THE REQUEST BY THE TRUSTEE OF THE CARR MCCLELLAN P.C. TO INSTRUCT THE RELEASE OF FUNDS IS GRANTED. AN ORDER RECALLING THE ISSUANCE OF BENCH WARRANT AND AN ORDER INSTRUCTING RELEASE OF FUNDS HELD IN CARR MCCLELLAN TRUST ACCOUNT TO JASON EVERETT THOMPSON WERE SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER IMPOSING THE MONETARY SANCTIONS FOR THE COURT'S SIGNATURE. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt
Off Calendar Nov-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jessica R. Barsotti, Attorney of Law, appearing with defendant Dean Gregory Asimos
Stephan E. Kyle of KYLE LAW CORPORATION, appearing for plaintiffs Jason Everett Thompson and Wired Real Estate Group, Inc.

RULING:
CONTINUED HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT

Hearing held. The Court heard and made the following ruling: (1) The Court finds that Counsel Jessica R. Barsotti and Defendant Dean Gregory Asimos did not appear at the November 13, 2015 hearing as ordered previously. Furthermore Defendant Asimos did not fully comply with the previous court order to produce the signed release of the funds held in the Carr McClellan Trust Account. The Court finds defendant Asimos in contempt for failure to appear and a violation of a Court order. (2) The Court orders the stay of the $1,000.00 contempt fine as to Mr. Dean Gregory Asimos lifted and the fine imposed as of this date. Defendant has until January 4, 2016 by 4:00 p.m. to deliver a check of $1,000 to the Clerk of the Superior Court, County of San Francisco. The Court, on its own motion, orders to recall of the Bench Warrant as to Mr. Asimos issued on November 17, 2015. (3) The request by the trustee of the Carr McClellan P.C. to instruct the release of funds is granted. An order recalling the issuance of Bench Warrant and an order instructing release of funds held in Carr McClellan Trust Account to Jason Everett Thompson were signed in open Court. Counsel for plaintiffs is to prepare an order imposing the monetary sanctions for the Court's signature. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

ORDSC DISPOSITION
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:

Stephan E. Kyle of KyleLaw Corporation (415.839.8110), appearing for plaintiff/opposing party.
Andrew H. Winetroub of KyleLaw Corporation (415.839.8192), appearing for plaintiff/moving party.

No appearance by any other party or counsel.

RULING:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT/STATUS REPORT REGARDING AUTHORIZATION.

Matter called. The Court addressed the non-appearance by the Defendant Dean Gregory Asimos and his counsel of record, Jessica R. Barsotti. The Court stated that it had received no communication from Ms. Barsotti or her client today. The clerk confirmed that there was no communication with Department 503 regarding the Defendant and his attorney's failure to appear today. Mr. Kyle stated that he had not heard from Ms. Barsotti and that their last communication was with regard to the authorization that he received on November 9, 2015. He had phoned Ms. Barsotti because the authorization was signed but NOT dated, that was their last communication. The Court lifted the stay of the $1000.00 contempt fine as to Mr. Asimos, and imposed the fine as of this date. The Court stated that the defendant and his attorney were ordered to appear today. The Court further stated that the Defendant Dean Gregory Asimos failed to appear at today's hearing, that it finds that this is a Criminal Contempt and orders a $50,000.00 bench warrant to issue for the Defendant Dean Gregory Asimos. The Court finds that this is a willful failure to appear and a violation of a Court order. Mr. Asimos' counsel, Jessica R. Barsotti, will be at a minimum, referred to the State Bar for a violation of a Court order and possible Order to Show Cause Re: Contempt. The Court noted that the matter before it today was set for 11:00 a.m. and that it was now 11:30 a.m. The Court stated that it would research whether or not the bench warrant and the order to show cause(s) re: contempt could be issued. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported. (503/TLJ)
